While both aortic stenosis and Eisenmenger's syndrome can significantly impact obstetric management requiring careful planning including possible cesarean delivery, aortic regurgitation tends to have milder hemodynamic effects without posing such high-risk scenarios justifying routine cesarean induction solely because of its nature.
